Its so hard I have a lot of breakdowns Ill never be that good It does feel that way when you start big art projects like a manga Some pro artists have more fun drawing smaller projects first like silly fun comics And then work up to bigger projects Its impressive that pewds can pull through but there are healthier ways to achieve drawing your manga without having to torture yourself and avoid burnout Focus on drawing a part of your manga everyday and the rest of the Manga will reveal itself over time  Also I dont think I noticed any manga tutorial books in your video The manga artists are really good inspiration but there are some good ones out there that can help you understand the fundementals

text:
Hey Pewds I was just thinking as I watched through your final manga that the most impressive thing from this is that you finished it just like that artist said to you Creative work really is about finishing the thing and moving on because each time you do you grow in a way that it is hard to perceive right away but that is always the key To finish and move on

I work in animation with storyboard and writting and here are my technical thoughts on it if it interests you Your drawings are super good and love your wide shots You could make use of the rule of thirdsless centered focus for a next one as that could bring a bit of variety and push the feeling you want to express especially when you do something purely visual no dialogue as this In terms of writing and the story your instinct to cut things is absolutely right  the best stories are told with a lot of subtext and implications rather than showing everything except for specific moments that deserve extra attention The ending felt like it needed that but some of the first few shots couldve been cut like you said

Its up to you if you want to take or leave my feedback but the last thing Id say from creator to creator is always listen to your gut Sometimes you cant explain it but that is part of the magic in art a lot of it is made by feeling what you should or should not do Again impressive work overall and especially that you push through it all and finished it I hope you take time to rest but then continue with your next artistic adventure because you obviously feel joy from it and have an artistic spirit
label: positive

text: Hey man you might not read this but here it goes anyways Im 38 almost 39 years old guy that has a wife an a 8 years old son So I think were pretty much from the same generation Ive followed you since 2012 more or less I somewhat felt like I envied you because you can do all this stuff that I always wanted to do but always had to put down or postpone indefinitely because I didnt have the time or whatever reason  however this video hit something different and I noticed you and I have something in common throughout the video you like criticized yourself or I noticed that you had your doubts or concerns but you kept forward you kept at it you had lows and ups but you kept pressing forward and you finished it You finished your own manga and thats no small feat considering you have a wife and a son and I guess a lot of other things to do but you kept pushing for something you wanted to do and I felt really happy for you by the end when you read your finished product And I could sense all the love and effort you put on and that got me thinking Theres always a phrase my wife tells me Better done than perfect You dont need it to be perfect you need it to be done So congratulations man You did really well and you should be proud of what you made I will try to do as well
label: positive
